mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is pinned near zero and CPIAUCSL is stable, discount rates stay compressed and equity valuations re-rate upward; this predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month as investors price continued accommodation.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not above the unconditional baseline when FEDFUNDS is at or below 0.25% and CPI inflation is not accelerating, or if triggered periods show negative average excess returns.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening household income growth and lower cyclical earnings expectations, which should reduce S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if episodes where UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in RSAFS relative to prior months signals strengthening consumer demand and better near-term revenue growth, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month annualized RSAFS growth does not coincide with above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign relationship flips for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in INDPRO and HOUST indicates broad real activity and capacity utilization are rising together, which supports earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising INDPRO and rising HOUST fails to outperform single-series signals, or if combined-positive episodes do not deliver positive average 20-day SPX excess returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in GDPC1 marks a macro demand shock that will be reflected in weaker corporate revenues and risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized quarterly growth falls below 1%
failure_condition: Reject if quarters with GDPC1 annualized growth below 1% are not followed by negative average 60-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate is indistinguishable from chance.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS keeps rising while UNRATE stops falling, labor market gains are losing momentum and margin-sensitive equity sectors should underperform, leading to weaker S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS positive 3-month trend while UNRATE is flat or higher
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up/UNRATE-flat-or-up condition does not precede negative 5-day SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the average return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in FEDFUNDS above neutral conditions raises the equity discount rate and tightens financial conditions, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next month once tightening is clearly underway.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not negative on average after FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.50 percentage points over 3 months, or if the sign does not remain stable across tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising INDPRO together with rising HOUST signals inventory rebuilding and cyclical output normalization, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the trio of rising INDPRO, rising HOUST, and rising PAYEMS does not produce positive average 60-day SPX returns, or if the conditional hit rate is below the unconditional baseline.
